Diary of a Wimpy Kid (2010)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $64,003,625Details
International Box Office $12,950,686
Worldwide Box Office $76,954,311
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $43,165,116
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $4,270,246
Total $47,435,362
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Diary of a Wimpy Kid chronicles the adventures of wisecracking pre-teen Greg Heffley, who must somehow survive the scariest time of anyone's life... middle school. Convinced it's the "dumbest idea ever invented," Heffley considers junior high school a place rigged with hundreds of social landmines, not the least of which are wedgies, swirlies, bullies, lunchtime banishment of the cafeteria floor and a festering piece of cheese with nuclear cooties that he must overcome to become popular. His diary - or "journal" - chronicles his thoughts, tales of family trials and tribulations, and (would be) schoolyard triumphs.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$22,126,166 (34.6% of total gross)
Legs:2.89 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:83.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$15,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,077 opening theaters/3,083 max. theaters, 6.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $97,019,436

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 19th, 2010 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
Video Release: August 3rd, 2010 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for some rude humor and language
(Rating bulletin 2107, 2/10/2010)
Running Time: 92 minutes
Franchise: Diary of a Wimpy Kid
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animated Sequences, Breaking the Fourth Wall
Plot point: Dysfunctional Family, New Guy/Girl in School, Sibling Rivalry, Wrestling
Social setting: Middle School
Subgenre: Family Comedy
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Walden Media
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Diary of a Wimpy Kid chronicles the adventures of wisecracking pre-teen Greg Heffley, who must somehow survive the scariest time of anyone's life... middle school. Convinced it's the "dumbest idea ever invented," Heffley considers junior high school a place rigged with hundreds of social landmines, not the least of which are wedgies, swirlies, bullies, lunchtime banishment of the cafeteria floor and a festering piece of cheese with nuclear cooties that he must overcome to become popular. His diary - or "journal" - chronicles his thoughts, tales of family trials and tribulations, and (would be) schoolyard triumphs.
